title: Suprathermal Ions
subtitle: Remnants of past energetic events
description: Suprathermal ions are particles with energies above the thermal solar wind but below fully accelerated cosmic rays. These remnant populations exhibit a universal spectral index of 2.5 that remains invariant across nine different elements and two full solar cycles, revealing a fundamental constraint on particle acceleration in the heliosphere.

related_figures.relevance: Validates that the quiet-time selection method produces consistent thresholds across two full solar cycles, ensuring that downstream suprathermal abundance measurements reflect real physical changes rather than methodological drift

Confirms that quiet hours anticorrelate with sunspot number at -0.95, validating the quiet-time identification as physically meaningful and establishing the 22-year baseline for studying suprathermal composition evolution
Proves suprathermals are remnants from CIRs during solar minimum and CME-driven shocks during solar maximum
Reduces the complex behavior of eight suprathermal elements to power-law relationships with a single enrichment indicator (Fe/C), revealing cyclic composition loops tied to solar activity phase
Connects suprathermals to their role as seed population for solar energetic particle events
Shows all 9 elements following the same spectral slope

related_topics.connection: Suprathermal ions are seed populations for SEP events
Suprathermal composition varies with the solar cycle

paper.title: Quiet-time Spectra of Suprathermal Heavy Ions near 1 au in Solar Cycles 23 and 24
